# The boot path & kernel-crash forensics 🔴

**Goal.** Own every stage from firmware to a captured kernel panic: interrupt the boot loader, force and dissect a crash, meet the firmware that answers back at a Forth prompt (twice — two rival implementations of the same standard), then replace the firmware's boot logic with Linux itself.

**Audience:** you can bring up a Debian VM; comfort with GRUB/serial helps  
**Effort:** ⏱ half-day+  
**Prerequisites:** none

| # | Lab | You'll learn | Checkpoint (observable) |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | [`vm-examples/vm-debian-amd64.toml`](../vm-examples/vm-debian-amd64.toml) | a baseline KVM cloud VM to experiment on. | `lab-vm.sh ssh` logs you in. |
| 2 | [`root-password-reset`](../root-password-reset/) 🔑 | console/boot access equals root — via `init=/bin/bash` or `rd.break`. | reset root on the VM; the OLD password is rejected and the NEW one works; `/proc/cmdline` proves your GRUB edit took (mind the serial char-drop gotcha). |
| 3 | [`kdump-kexec-lab`](../kdump-kexec-lab/) | arm kdump, force a panic, let the kexec capture kernel save a vmcore, then read it in `crash`. | `echo c > /proc/sysrq-trigger` yields a ~42 MB vmcore in `/var/crash`; `crash` `sym` resolves the panic to `test-module.c:8`. |
| 4 | [`open-firmware-forth-to-boot`](../open-firmware-forth-to-boot/) 📟 | the rival answer to firmware modularity, live: IEEE 1275's `ok` prompt is a Forth REPL on the bare machine — walk the device tree, patch the running firmware, and make it boot Linux; also as a coreboot payload. | `3 4 + .` prints `7` at the `ok` prompt over serial (`smoke-ofw.sh` PASSes both flavors); `showcase-forth-to-boot.sh` ends `PASS: ... booted Linux to u-root` after hand-staging the initrd at the prompt. |
| 5 | [`openbios-the-rival-that-shipped`](../openbios-the-rival-that-shipped/) ☎️ | the SAME standard, the OTHER implementation — OpenBIOS, the one still shipping (QEMU's default ppc/sparc firmware). The contrast IS the lesson: where the frozen rival got fixed live at the prompt, this maintained C loader gets fixed in a patch — booting Linux needs no `memmap=`, no hand-staged initrd, no zero-page poke. | `smoke-openbios.sh` PASSes all three tracks (multiboot/coreboot/ppc); the ppc one proves the running firmware is OUR build (banner date ≠ QEMU's bundled blob); `showcase-rival-boots-linux.sh` ends `PASS: the rival boots Linux ... reached u-root` from one `boot` line. |
| 6 | [`linuxboot-uefi-kexec`](../linuxboot-uefi-kexec/) | firmware is just software: u-root as the firmware's `init`, which `kexec`s the real OS. | two 'Welcome to u-root!' banners + a `[0.000000]` clock reset (Tier C fast-loop → Tier B UKI on OVMF → Tier A coreboot ROM); the System-Transparency capstone boots ONLY a signed OSPKG and refuses a rogue one. |

### Optional side-quests

- [`systemd261-nixos-measured-boot`](../systemd261-nixos-measured-boot/) — boot *integrity* rather than boot *recovery*: a Nix-built NixOS on **systemd 261** measured into a (soft)TPM, deployed on-disk over iPXE, with staged rollout by machine-ID (`ConditionFraction=`) and storage sealed to the measured state (TPM2 LUKS bound to PCR 7+11 + a PCR-quote attestation stub). Spikes A–G verified — measured-os MET on a dm-verity+UKI image, sealed LUKS unseals only on the expected PCRs; honest gap: `RestrictFileSystemAccess=` isn't in nixpkgs' systemd 261. See its PLAN.md.
